title: Knowledge Base (RAG): Teach Your Agent Your Business
lede: Upload your FAQs, price lists, policies, and manuals once — and your AI agent will pull accurate answers from them on every call, chat, and message. This guide walks you from your first upload to a fully knowledge-powered agent.

steps.title: Open the Knowledge Base Page
steps.content:
The Knowledge Base is your company's document library. Every file you upload here can be attached to any of your agents.

  1. In the top navigation, click the Contact Center section.
  2. In the sub-tab slider below it, click Knowledge Base. (You can also use the left sidebar: under Call Center, click KnowledgeBase.)
  3. You land on the Knowledge Base page with a search bar, a Supported File Types panel, and a table of all uploaded files.
💡 The Knowledge Base is shared across your whole account — upload a file once and attach it to as many agents as you like.

images.alt: Knowledge Base page with the Add Files button, supported file types panel, and files table
images.caption: The Knowledge Base page — your document library for all agents.

images.alt: Knowledge Base page with the Add Files button, supported file types panel, and files table
images.caption: The Knowledge Base page — your document library for all agents.

steps.title: Know What You Can Upload
steps.content:
iMash accepts far more than PDFs. The Supported File Types panel on the page lists every accepted format:

  • Documents — .pdf, .txt, .docx, .doc, .rtf (up to 50 MB)
  • Spreadsheets — .xlsx, .xls, .csv (up to 25 MB)
  • Presentations — .pptx, .ppt (up to 100 MB)
  • Images — .jpg, .png, .gif, .webp and more (up to 20 MB) — text is extracted automatically with OCR, so even a photographed menu or flyer works
  • Web & Markup — .html, .xml, .md (up to 5 MB)
  • Data — .json, .jsonl (up to 10 MB)
  • eBooks — .epub (up to 50 MB)
  • Code — .py, .js, .ts, .sql, .yaml and a dozen more
⚠️ Files above the size limit for their category are rejected during validation — split very large documents into smaller topic files (which also improves answer quality).

images.alt: Supported File Types panel listing documents, spreadsheets, presentations, images, web, data, ebooks and code formats
images.caption: Every format the Knowledge Base accepts, grouped by category.

images.alt: Supported File Types panel listing documents, spreadsheets, presentations, images, web, data, ebooks and code formats
images.caption: Every format the Knowledge Base accepts, grouped by category.

steps.title: Upload Your First File
steps.content:
1. Click Add Files in the top-right corner of the page.
  2. Pick one or more files from your computer (you can select several at once).
  3. iMash validates each file, uploads it, and shows "Embedding started in background" — that means the platform is reading your document and indexing its contents so your agent can search it.
  4. Watch the Status column on the file's row:
    • Started (blue, spinning) — the file is being processed. Small files take seconds; large ones a few minutes.
    • Enabled (green) — the file is fully indexed and ready to attach to agents.
    • Disabled (gray) — the file is stored but not indexed. Click Enable in the Actions column to index it.
💡 The page refreshes the status automatically every few seconds while a file is processing — no need to reload.

images.alt: A file row showing size, created date, owner pill, green Enabled status and the Preview, Enable/Disable and Delete actions
images.caption: A processed file: green Enabled status means it is ready for your agents.

images.alt: A file row showing size, created date, owner pill, green Enabled status and the Preview, Enable/Disable and Delete actions
images.caption: A processed file: green Enabled status means it is ready for your agents.

steps.title: Understand the Files Table
steps.content:
Each row in the table tells you everything about a file at a glance:

  • File — name and type icon. Use the Search bar above the table to filter by name.
  • Size and Created — upload size and date.
  • Owner — who uploaded it: Main Account (shared tenant file), Sub … (uploaded by a specific sub-account), or Own (your personal upload when signed in as a sub-account).
  • Status — Enabled, Started, or Disabled (see the previous step).
  • Assistants — pills naming every agent currently using this file, so you always know what a change will affect.
  • Actions — Preview, a download icon, Enable/Disable, and a red delete icon.
⚠️ Disable removes the file's search index AND detaches it from every agent listed in the Assistants column (a confirmation asks first). Delete removes the file entirely. If you only want to swap a document for a newer version, delete the old file, upload the new one, and re-attach it to your agents.

steps.title: Preview and Download Files
steps.content:
Not sure what's inside a file you uploaded months ago? Check it without leaving the page:

  1. Click Preview in the file's Actions column.
  2. A File Preview drawer opens showing the document's contents, its type, size, and owner.
  3. From here you can click Download to save a copy, Copy Link to copy a temporary signed link to the file, or Delete to remove it.
  4. Close the drawer with the X in the top-left corner.

images.alt: File Preview drawer showing the contents of an FAQ text file with Download, Copy Link and Delete buttons
images.caption: The File Preview drawer — read, download, or share any file in place.

images.alt: File Preview drawer showing the contents of an FAQ text file with Download, Copy Link and Delete buttons
images.caption: The File Preview drawer — read, download, or share any file in place.

steps.title: Open Your Agent's Knowledge Base Panel
steps.content:
Files don't help until an agent can use them. Time to connect the two:

  1. In the top navigation, click Automation, then the Smart Agents sub-tab (or click Smart Agents in the left sidebar under Intelligence Center).
  2. Click your agent's name to open the agent editor.
  3. In the middle column, find Knowledge Base — it is the very first panel, with an ON/OFF toggle on the right showing whether any files are attached.
💡 Working in the Node editor view instead? The Knowledge Base lives there too, as a panel with an Open knowledge base button — the file picker that opens is identical.

images.alt: Agent editor form view with the Knowledge Base panel at the top of the middle column
images.caption: The agent editor — Knowledge Base sits at the top of the middle column.

images.alt: Agent editor form view with the Knowledge Base panel at the top of the middle column
images.caption: The agent editor — Knowledge Base sits at the top of the middle column.

images.alt: Knowledge Base panel header with its OFF toggle
images.caption: Click this header to choose which files the agent can search.

images.alt: Knowledge Base panel header with its OFF toggle
images.caption: Click this header to choose which files the agent can search.

steps.title: Attach Files to the Agent
steps.content:
1. Click the Knowledge Base panel header. The Select Knowledge Base Files window opens with the same files table you saw on the Knowledge Base page.
  2. Tick the checkbox next to each file this agent should know. Only files with an Enabled status can be selected — a grayed-out checkbox means the file still needs to be enabled (hover it to see the hint "Enable this file first").
  3. Click Save Selection. The panel toggle flips to ON and the header shows how many files are attached, e.g. (2 selected).
  4. To detach files later, reopen the picker and untick them, or switch the panel toggle to OFF to clear the whole selection at once.
💡 Each agent gets its own selection. Give your sales agent the price list and your support agent the troubleshooting manual — from the same shared library.

images.alt: Select Knowledge Base Files window with a file checked and the Save Selection button
images.caption: Tick the files your agent should know, then click Save Selection.

images.alt: Select Knowledge Base Files window with a file checked and the Save Selection button
images.caption: Tick the files your agent should know, then click Save Selection.

steps.title: How the Agent Uses It Mid-Call
steps.content:
This is where the magic happens. Attached files power Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG):

  • When a caller asks something — "What are your hours?", "Do you service my zip code?" — the agent instantly searches its attached files for the most relevant passages.
  • It weaves what it finds into a natural spoken answer, in real time, without the caller noticing any lookup.
  • Only the relevant snippets are used, so the agent stays fast and accurate even with large documents attached.

This also keeps your system prompt lean: instead of pasting your entire FAQ into the prompt, keep the prompt short (the editor recommends staying under ~3,000 tokens) and let the Knowledge Base carry the reference material.

steps.title: Test That It Works
steps.content:
1. In the agent editor, click Test Agent (top-right) or Connect in the chat panel on the right.
  2. Ask questions your document answers — business hours, prices, warranty terms, service area.
  3. Confirm the agent replies with the facts from your file, not generic guesses.
  4. If it doesn't pull from the file, add a nudge to the prompt, for example: "When asked a question, answer using the FAQ in your knowledge base."

steps.title: Write Documents Your Agent Will Love
steps.content:
Great source documents make the difference between a good agent and a great one:

  • Question & answer format wins. An FAQ file ("Q: … A: …") maps perfectly to how callers actually ask.
  • One topic per file. Split "Everything.pdf" into "Pricing", "Service Areas", and "Warranty" — retrieval stays focused and answers get sharper.
  • Be explicit and self-contained. Write "The diagnostic visit costs $89" rather than "see table above" — the agent retrieves passages, not whole pages.
  • Keep it current. When prices or hours change, delete the outdated file, upload the new version, and re-attach it — an agent quoting last year's prices is worse than one that says "let me check".
  • Prune ruthlessly. Remove marketing fluff and internal notes; every irrelevant paragraph competes with the facts you want retrieved.

That's it — your agent now answers with your knowledge, on every channel, around the clock. 🎉

support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials
